tasks.title: Remove repeated dashboard token details
tasks.objective: Audit dashboard content and remove repeated token information where row-level copy duplicates the balance summary.
authority: Remove duplicate presentation copy without changing ledger calculations or event status behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard balance information is not repeated in each cohort/event row when the summary already communicates it.
Cohort/event rows still show enough status context to explain access, quorum, and participation state.
Tests no longer assert duplicated row-level token summaries.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when duplicated dashboard token copy is removed and tests cover the revised content.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 10:06 EDT: Removed row-level creator/participant token copy from dashboard cards while preserving topic, schedule, quorum, status, and link context. Covered by dashboard and MVP verification tests.

tasks.title: Simplify dashboard token summary states
tasks.objective: Update dashboard token summary presentation to show only Available, In use, and Used.
authority: Keep refund/returned values available in the ledger and services, but do not render a Returned dashboard summary section.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard token summary contains Available, In use, and Used sections.
Dashboard token summary does not render a Returned section.
Refunded token accounting remains unchanged in persistence and ledger tests.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when Returned is removed from dashboard presentation while internal refund semantics remain untouched.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 10:06 EDT: Dashboard token panels now render only Available, In use, and Used. Ledger refund accounting remains unchanged and tests assert Returned is absent from dashboard output.

tasks.title: Rename dashboard sections around user intent
tasks.objective: Replace labels like Creator dashboard and Participant dashboard with friendlier section names based on what the page contains.
authority: Prefer user-facing labels such as My Cohorts and My Events when they match the displayed content.
Consider Created Cohorts, Interested Cohorts, and Active Cohorts & Schedule as the preferred content model.
Preserve existing routes unless a separate navigation issue explicitly changes them.
acceptance_criteria: Combined dashboard no longer presents its main panels as Creator dashboard and Participant dashboard.
Dashboard labels communicate created cohorts, interested cohorts, and active cohorts/schedules in user-friendly terms.
Entry links and post-action links use consistent dashboard wording.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when dashboard wording is consistently updated without breaking legacy route compatibility.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 10:06 EDT: Replaced Creator/Participant dashboard presentation labels with My Cohorts, My Events, My Cohorts & Events, Account Tokens, Created Cohorts, and Interested Cohorts. Legacy routes remain covered by tests.

tasks.title: Review dashboard user flow hierarchy
tasks.objective: Review the dashboard page flow and adjust hierarchy, empty states, and next actions so the page reads clearly from account state to cohort/event activity.
authority: Adjust dashboard layout and copy to improve scan order while preserving MVP functionality.
acceptance_criteria: The dashboard has a clear first-read hierarchy.
Users can distinguish token availability, active cohorts/schedules, created cohorts, interested cohorts, and available next actions.
Empty states point to the appropriate next action without using internal role labels.
verification: Browser smoke test `/dashboard` if a dev server is already running or can be started
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when the dashboard flow review changes are implemented and verified.
tasks.evidence: 2026-05-30 10:06 EDT: Combined dashboard now leads with account token state, then Active Cohorts & Schedule, then Created Cohorts and Interested Cohorts with clearer empty states and next-action copy. Verified by tests, lint, and browser smoke.
